title: oxalis monophylla var. monophylla
intro: Oxalis monophylla var. monophylla, also known as the One-leaf Sorrel or Pygmy Wood Sorrel, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America, specifically in the Pacific Northwest. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its high concentration of oxalic acid and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxalis monophylla var. monophylla include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
